relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The people are told to carry the whole command across the river.
Great stones and white plaster prepare a public surface for Torah.
The words must travel into the land before possession becomes identity.
Ebal receives the inscribed stones.
An altar is built without iron shaping its stones.
Whole stones carry offerings before YHWH.
Covenant is eaten as peace and rejoicing, not only pronounced as law.
The words are written plainly enough to be publicly known.
Silence and hearing mark renewed peoplehood.
Belonging becomes listening and practice.
Moses arranges the people across two mountains.
Gerizim holds the tribes appointed for blessing.
Ebal holds the tribes appointed for curse.
Levites speak loudly enough for every person to answer.
Secretly manufactured worship is placed beneath communal refusal.
Contempt for parents is named publicly, though honor can never require submission to abuse.
Moving a boundary stone is exposed as theft of another's future.
Deliberately misleading a blind traveler becomes a covenant offense.
The court is cursed when it bends against immigrant, orphan, or widow.
A father's household boundary is protected from sexual violation.
Sexual use of an animal is rejected.
Incestuous access to a sister is rejected.
The mother-in-law relationship is also protected.
Secret assault is brought into public condemnation.
A bribe that purchases innocent blood is cursed before all.
The final Amen binds knowledge of Torah to doing it.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 27 makes hidden abuse answerable in public. Boundary theft, disability endangerment, corrupt courts, sexual violation, secret assault, and paid killing are all dragged into the open and met with communal refusal.
